mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which improves near-term corporate earnings forecasts and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO trends upward, or the signal fails to predict direction in >70% of recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy compresses real discount rates and corporate profit margins, creating a stagflationary environment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive when both proxies exceed thresholds, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Robust quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3 percent signals strong aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are negative in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3 percent, or the predictive IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth, or the signal produces negative returns in >65% of observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising interest rates signals an economic slowdown exacerbated by monetary tightening, reducing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is below 2 percent.
